items.summary: The City Manager requested an appropriation of $5,234,379 from Free Cash to cover the cost of snow operations during the winter season. Adopted 8-0-1 with Councillor Simmons absent.

Adopted unanimously and placed on file.

items.title: Raise residential parking permit fee to $75 (subsidized $25 for low-income / SNAP / affordable housing); remove senior exemption; cap permits at 2/household
items.summary: A previously tabled policy order proposing major changes to the residential parking permit program: a $75 base fee, a $25 subsidized fee for residents in affordable housing, on SNAP, or low-income, removal of the senior exemption, and a cap of 2 permits per household (down from 4). Item remained on the table at this meeting and No Action was Taken at the March 30, 2026 meeting.
items.body: That the City Manager is requested to work with relevant City departments to raise the fee of the parking permit program for all residents to $75, consider how to include a self-identified check off option so as not to increase administrative costs for a subsidized fee of $25 for residents who live in affordable housing, are enrolled in a program such as SNAP or are low income, remove the senior exemption for the residential parking permit program and lower the number of cars that individual residents are allowed to get a residential parking permit for from four to two.

history.action: Charter Right Exercised by Councillor Simmons

rationale.doc.meeting_date:2026-03-23: appears on this meeting's agenda/final actions calendar
rationale.doc.status:no_action: NO ACTION TAKEN IN COUNCIL MARCH 30, 2026
rationale.doc.stage:tabled: PLACED ON THE TABLE IN COUNCIL MARCH 2, 2026
rationale.doc.stage:carryover: originally filed earlier and Charter-Righted then tabled across meetings
rationale.doc.sponsor:simmons: Charter Right Exercised by Councillor Simmons (and is the original sponsor handler in carryover record)
rationale.housing.tenure:any: residential parking permits affect both renters and owners
rationale.housing.affordability:inclusionary: residents who live in affordable housing
rationale.fiscal.kind:parking_fee: raise the fee of the parking permit program
rationale.demo.age:senior_65plus: remove the senior exemption for the residential parking permit program
rationale.demo.income:low: subsidized fee of $25 for residents who ... are low income
rationale.demo.income:means_tested: enrolled in a program such as SNAP

items.title: Support H.91 and S.2556, An Act to modernize funding for community media programming

items.title: Meet with Harvard and Longwood Collective (MASCO) to harmonize institutional bus protocols with public commitments
items.summary: A policy order asking the Community Development and Transportation Departments to meet with Harvard's Office of Community Relations and the Longwood Collective (MASCO) so that institutional shuttle-bus operations match the institutions' public-facing commitments to neighbors. Adopted unanimously.

items.title: Study requiring single-stall public bathrooms to be gender-neutral
items.summary: A policy order asking the City Manager to direct relevant City departments to examine and report back on whether Cambridge can require single-stall public bathrooms to be gender-neutral. Adopted as amended unanimously.
items.body: That the City Manager is requested to direct relevant City departments and staff to examine and report back on whether the city can require single-stall public bathrooms to be gender-neutral.
amendments: Adopted as Amended (text of amendments not in agenda/final actions excerpt)

items.title: Zoning 4.50 amended: religious, qualifying educational, and child-care uses as-of-right in all districts
items.summary: Amends Section 4.50 of the Cambridge Zoning Ordinance to permit religious uses, qualifying educational uses (on land owned/leased by the Commonwealth, religious bodies, or nonprofit educational corporations), and child-care uses (centers, school-age programs, family child care homes) as-of-right in every zoning district. Ordained 8-1 with Councillor Flaherty as the lone nay vote.
items.body: AMEND SECTION 4.50 OF THE CAMBRIDGE ZONING ORDINANCE AS FOLLOWS WITH THE INTENT OF PERMITTING THE USE OF LAND FOR THE FOLLOWING PURPOSES AS-OF-RIGHT IN ALL ZONING DISTRICTS: RELIGIOUS PURPOSES; EDUCATIONAL PURPOSES ON LAND OWNED OR LEASED BY THE COMMONWEALTH OR ANY OF ITS AGENCIES, SUBDIVISIONS OR BODIES POLITIC OR BY A RELIGIOUS SECT OR DENOMINATION, OR BY A NONPROFIT EDUCATIONAL CORPORATION; AND FOR A CHILD CARE CENTER, SCHOOL-AGED CHILD CARE PROGRAM, FAMILY CHILD CARE HOME, OR LARGE FAMILY CHILD CARE HOME AS DEFINED IN SECTION 1A OF CHAPTER 15D OF MASSACHUSETTS GENERAL LAWS.

history.action: Pass to a Second Reading in Council

rationale.doc.stage:second_reading: PASS TO A SECOND READING IN COUNCIL MARCH 2, 2026; ELIGIBLE TO BE ORDAINED MARCH 23, 2026
rationale.housing.tenure:any: zoning amendment affects all property regardless of tenure
rationale.household.kids:under_5: CHILD CARE CENTER, FAMILY CHILD CARE HOME
rationale.topic.area:housing_zoning: amendment to the Cambridge Zoning Ordinance
rationale.topic.area:schools: covers educational uses and school-age care

items.title: Awaiting Report: open city-owned/school parking lots (incl. Tobin/Darby Vassal) for after-hours resident use
items.summary: An awaiting-report item asking the City Manager to work with the School Department, DPW and others to make city-owned parking lots — focused on school complexes including the under-construction Tobin/Darby Vassal — available for after-hours use by residents.
items.body: That the City Manager is requested to work with the School Department, the Department of Public Works, and other relevant departments to ensure that all city owned parking lots, with a focus on school complexes, including the still under construction parking at Tobin/Darby Vassal school complex, could be made available for after-hours use by residents. Original Awaiting Report Item AR-25-29.

rationale.geo.scope:school_zone: names a specific school complex (Tobin/Darby Vassal)
rationale.transport.subject:parking: city owned parking lots ... after-hours use
rationale.household.school:cps: Tobin/Darby Vassal is a CPS school complex

items.title: Awaiting Report: formal policy on City role/financial responsibility for large public events by Cambridge nonprofits
items.summary: An awaiting-report item asking the City Manager (with City Solicitor and other departments) to set a formal policy clearly defining the City's role and financial responsibilities when supporting large-scale public events hosted by Cambridge-based nonprofit organizations.
items.body: The City Manager is requested to consult with the appropriate City departments, including the City Solicitor's Office, to establish a formal policy that clearly defines the City's role and financial responsibilities in supporting large-scale public events hosted by Cambridge-based non-profit and not-for-profit organizations. Original Awaiting Report Item AR-25-48.

rationale.topic.area:arts: large public events often cultural/arts events
rationale.topic.area:small_business: policy clarifies city support touching small-business event ecosystem
rationale.work.employer:nonprofit: large-scale public events hosted by Cambridge-based non-profit and not-for-profit organizations

items.title: Awaiting Report: community input and outreach to condo owners and short-term rental operators
items.summary: An awaiting-report item asking the City Manager to work with departments to seek community input and direct outreach to condo owners and short-term rental operators, and to develop additional recommendations based on Ordinance Committee discussion.
items.body: The City Manager is requested to work with all relevant departments to seek input from the community as well as direct outreach to condo owners and short-term rental operators and develop additional recommendations based on the discussion in the Ordinance Committee. Original Awaiting Report Item AR-25-51.

rationale.geo.scope:citywide: outreach to condo owners and STR operators citywide

items.summary: An awaiting-report item asking staff to review the prior home rule petition and prepare a new one allowing Cambridge to enact a Real Estate Transfer Fee, to be sent to the state legislature.
items.body: Review the previous home rule petition and prepare a new petition that would allow Cambridge to enact a Real Estate Transfer Fee to be sent to the state legislature. Original Awaiting Report AR-25-69.

rationale.housing.tenure:rent: new TOD developments are largely rental units affecting renters

items.title: Awaiting Report: City funding for Tourism Destination Marketing District and oversight of visitor information kiosk
items.summary: An awaiting-report item asking the City Solicitor and Community Development Department to report on legal parameters around City funding and Tourism Destination Marketing District restrictions, and on oversight and accountability for the visitor information kiosk.
items.body: Direct the City Solicitor and the Community Development Department to review and report back to the Council on (i) legal parameters related to City funding and Tourism Destination Marketing District restrictions, and (ii) oversight and accountability related to the operation of the visitor information kiosk, and related governance expectations, including alignment with City priorities. Original Awaiting Report ID #31841.

rationale.topic.area:small_business: Tourism Destination Marketing District serves local businesses

items.title: Awaiting Report: prepare Council discussion on future uses for City-owned properties and redevelopment processes

rationale.housing.tenure:any: redevelopment can produce any tenure type

items.title: Awaiting Report: pilot pedestrianization projects in 2026; future Brattle St / JFK St pedestrianization study
items.summary: An awaiting-report item asking the City Manager to prioritize pilot pedestrianization projects in 2026 without a consultant-led study, and to consider further pedestrianization of a section of Brattle Street by restoring two-way traffic to JFK Street.
items.body: That the City Manager work with relevant City departments to prioritize pilot projects in 2026 without the use of a consultant for an extensive pedestrianization study, and in the medium-term consider how additional pedestrianization of a section of Brattle Street by restoring two-way traffic to JFK Street could enhance the Square.

rationale.geo.neighborhood:mid_cambridge: Harvard Square area is in Mid-Cambridge

items.title: Awaiting Report: comprehensive review of safety and security measures at Cambridge City Hall
items.summary: An awaiting-report item asking the City Manager to conduct a comprehensive review of existing safety and security measures at Cambridge City Hall — physical access controls, visitor screening, on-site security staffing, emergency-response protocols, and security technologies.
items.body: That the City Manager conduct a comprehensive review of existing safety and security measures at Cambridge City Hall, including an assessment of physical access controls, visitor screening practices, on-site security staffing and training, emergency response and evacuation protocols, and the use of security technologies such as surveillance systems and alarm monitoring.

items.title: Awaiting Report: feasibility of permitting third-party guarantors / co-signers for Inclusionary Housing applicants
items.summary: An awaiting-report item asking the Community Development Department to examine whether qualified third-party guarantors or co-signers could be permitted for applicants to Inclusionary Housing units, and whether the policy could responsibly expand access.
items.body: The City Manager is requested to direct the Community Development Department (CDD) to examine the feasibility, legal considerations, and administrative requirements of permitting qualified third-party guarantors or co-signers for applicants to Inclusionary Housing units and provide recommendations as to whether such a policy could responsibly expand access to these units.

rationale.housing.tenure:rent: Inclusionary Housing units are predominantly rental

items.title: Awaiting Report: draft ordinance restricting City property/personnel from federal civil immigration enforcement (Welcoming Community Ordinance)
items.summary: An awaiting-report item asking the Law Department to draft ordinance language restricting the use of City-owned/controlled property, facilities, resources, and personnel for federal civil immigration enforcement, and to evaluate whether to add these protections to the Welcoming Community Ordinance.
items.body: The City Manager is requested to direct the Law Department to draft ordinance language restricting the use of City-owned or City-controlled property, facilities, resources, and personnel for the purposes of federal civil immigration enforcement, and that the Law Department evaluate whether these protections should be adopted as an amendment to the Welcoming Community Ordinance.

rationale.topic.area:immigration: federal civil immigration enforcement / Welcoming Community Ordinance

items.title: Awaiting Report: review and update Cambridge digital equity / internet access programs (report by June 2026)
items.summary: An awaiting-report item asking departments to review the City's digital equity work and existing internet access programs, evaluate whether residents' digital needs are being met, and report back by June 2026.
items.body: The City Manager is requested to direct the appropriate departments to review the city's digital equity work to date, including the study's recommendations and all steps taken since the study conclusion in order to update the evaluation of existing internet access programs, assessing whether residents' digital needs are or could be better provided for and to propose how we can better meet their needs and to report back to the Council by June 2026 on internet access programs.

rationale.geo.scope:citywide: digital-equity programs serve the whole city
rationale.topic.area:civil_liberties: digital equity is an information-access civil-liberties concern
rationale.demo.income:low: digital equity programs primarily target lower-income residents

items.title: Awaiting Report: improve consistency of how meeting information is posted/accessed on City website
items.summary: An awaiting-report item asking the City Manager to assess opportunities to improve how meeting information is posted and accessed on the City's website.
items.body: That the City Manager is requested to assess opportunities to improve the consistency of how meeting information is posted and accessed on the City's website.

rationale.civic.flag:first_timer_friendly: improving meeting-info accessibility helps first-time engagers

items.title: Awaiting Report: consolidated capital lifecycle reconciliation for major capital initiatives prior to FY27 Capital Budget
items.summary: An awaiting-report item asking the City Manager to produce a consolidated capital lifecycle reconciliation for all major capital initiatives, to be delivered to the Council before adoption of the FY27 Capital Budget.
items.body: That the City Manager be and hereby is requested to produce and deliver to the City Council, prior to the adoption of the FY27 Capital Budget, a consolidated capital lifecycle reconciliation for all major capital initiatives.

items.title: Awaiting Report: explore a potential Cambridge Snow Corps program
items.summary: An awaiting-report item asking the City Manager to confer with relevant City staff and report back with information on exploration of a potential Cambridge Snow Corps program (volunteer-style sidewalk snow clearing). Report Accepted and Placed on File 8-0-1 (Councillor Simmons absent).
items.body: That the City Manager is requested to confer with relevant City staff and report back with information on the exploration of a potential Cambridge Snow Corps program.

vote.result: Report Accepted and Placed on File [8-0-1]

rationale.doc.status:report_accepted: RESULT: Report Accepted and Placed on File [8-0-1]
rationale.demo.age:senior_65plus: Snow Corps programs typically aid seniors who can't shovel
rationale.transport.accessibility:mobility: clear sidewalks affect residents with mobility limits

items.title: Awaiting Report: simplify the City's processes for paying election workers
items.summary: An awaiting-report item asking the City Manager to work with the Election Commissioners to review IRS guidance and consult with other municipalities to simplify the City's processes for paying election workers.
items.body: That the City Manager is requested to work with relevant City departments as well as the Election Commissioners to review guidance from the IRS and consult with other municipalities in order to simplify our processes for paying election workers.

rationale.civic.flag:voter_action_required: concerns staffing of polling places that voters interact with

items.title: Awaiting Report: expand free early child care offerings, including means-tested models and non-City funding
items.summary: An awaiting-report item asking the City Manager to work with departments on expanding free early child care offerings in Cambridge, including means-tested programming and non-City funding sources from the state or foundations.
items.body: That the City Manager is requested to work with the City Council and relevant City departments on the process by which Cambridge can expand free early child care offerings, including models for means-tested programming, and exploration of non-City funding sources, from the state or foundations.

rationale.geo.scope:citywide: early child care is a citywide service
rationale.demo.age:family: early child care directly affects families with young children

items.title: Awaiting Report: explore all steps toward advancing social housing in Cambridge
items.summary: An awaiting-report item asking the City Manager to confer with the Community Development, Finance, Law, and other departments to explore all steps toward advancing social housing in Cambridge.
items.body: That the City Manager be and hereby is requested to confer with the Community Development Department, Finance Department, Law Department, and other relevant departments to explore all steps towards advancing social housing in Cambridge. Original Awaiting Report Item AR-25-58.

rationale.geo.scope:citywide: social housing initiative would be citywide
rationale.housing.tenure:rent: social housing is publicly held rental housing
rationale.housing.affordability:public_housing: social housing is a public-housing model
rationale.topic.area:housing_zoning: advancing social housing is a housing policy item

items.title: Finance Committee report: status of Federal Grants (incl. ARPA) and Federal Grant Stabilization Fund; future Budget Priorities
items.summary: The Finance Committee report from a Feb. 25, 2026 public hearing on the status of Federal Grants (including ARPA), the Federal Grant Stabilization Fund, and an early discussion on Budget Priorities. Report accepted and placed on file unanimously.
items.body: The Finance Committee held a public hearing on February 25, 2026 to receive a status update on Federal Grants (including ARPA) and the Federal Grant Stabilization Fund, as well as a discussion on establishing future Budget Priorities.

vote.result: Report Accepted and Placed on File [9-0-0]

history.action: Report Accepted and Placed on File
